title: polygala patagonica
intro: Polygala patagonica, also known as Yareta or Azores, is a type of succulent plant that belongs to the family Polygalaceae. It is native to the Andes mountains in South America and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Polygala patagonica include reducing inflammation, alleviating coughs, and improving respiratory function. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat respiratory problems and support overall lung health.
